dataset: GS LinkOps AI auto-scan candidate records

records.title: Edge-side residual timing and frequency control for software-defined 5G NTN ground stations
records.summary: A 2026 hardware-in-the-loop study places residual timing-advance and carrier-frequency-offset control at the ground-station edge after coarse UE-side geometric pre-compensation. The design is relevant to rapidly varying LEO 5G NTN uplinks because the ground segment must continuously correct residual Doppler and propagation timing rather than relying only on static link configuration.

extracted_capabilities.architecture: UE coarse pre-compensation plus edge-side residual TA/CFO closed loop
extracted_capabilities.reported_test_context: March 2026 hardware-in-the-loop campaign across four ground-station locations

reported_results.note: Results are study-specific and must not be treated as a commercial SLA or general performance guarantee.

records.confidence_basis: Recent primary research with hardware-in-the-loop evidence; external commercial implementation and repeatability remain unverified.
pending_confirmation: Controller update rate and stability limits
Compatibility with commercial SDRs, modems and digitizers
Clock source, PTP/GNSS requirements and holdover behavior
Performance under rain fade, interference and packet loss

sources.title: Edge-Side Residual Timing and Frequency Control for Software-Defined Ground Stations in 5G NTN Uplinks

records.title: Cloud-based on-demand satellite operation with multi-ground-station automation
records.summary: A 2026 operational report from Tohoku University describes cloud-based management of satellites and ground stations, including automatic command generation. By 2022, the reported system was managing up to eight satellites daily through three ground stations in Sendai, Hakodate and Sweden. The record is useful for modeling mission-control automation separately from RF station capability.

extracted_capabilities.operation_model: cloud-based on-demand satellite and ground-station operations
extracted_capabilities.reported_satellite_scale: up to 8 operational satellites simultaneously managed on a daily basis

records.confidence_basis: Primary operational report describes real mission use, but detailed interfaces, software architecture and commercial availability are not fully public.
pending_confirmation: Ground-station antenna, band and baseband configurations
Command authentication and human approval controls
API, message bus and scheduler interface specifications
Failure handling, rollback and safe-mode procedures
Whether the Sweden station is owned, leased or accessed through a partner

sources.title: Report on Earth Observation Missions and Ground Station Management using On-Demand Satellite Operation System

records.title: NGSO gateway planning using rain, visibility, elevation and traffic-demand constraints
records.summary: A gateway-planning study for non-geostationary constellations combines rain attenuation, minimum elevation, satellite visibility, geographic constraints and user-traffic demand into a map-grid site-selection method. It provides a useful planning layer between orbital coverage analysis and construction or GSaaS procurement decisions.

extracted_capabilities.planning_target: NGSO gateway station placement and network dimensioning

records.confidence_basis: Primary systems research with explicit planning criteria; actual deployment decisions require current local regulatory, RF, land, power and backhaul validation.
pending_confirmation: Current rain-climate dataset and percentile used
Customer traffic forecast and peak-to-average ratio

sources.title: Gateway Station Geographical Planning for Emerging Non-Geostationary Satellites Constellations

processing_notes: These records are candidate research inputs and are not operational capacity commitments.
Study-specific performance figures must be retained with source, scenario and validation context.
Before production import, deduplicate schema fields against prior AUTO-SCAN records and normalize sources into the common evidence table.
